issue.author: kpdev

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
Thanks for sharing the test case!

I tried to add it to the llvm-test-suite runtime tests, but it seems to pass on X86: https://reviews.llvm.org/D132551
 
Any ideas what could be going on?

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: @tstellar could you help with merging the PR (https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project-release-prs/pull/133) to fix this issue on the release branch? It looks like all tests have passed but it didn't get merged automatically.

comments.author: tru
comments.body:
@fhahn Tom is on vacation for the next 2 months. I am the release manager for 15.x. 

This seems like a big diff - is it safe to go into 15.0.0-final next week? We don't have any more RCs planned, so unless this is a critical blocker I would rather wait until 15.0.1 in three weeks.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
I think it should be safe; it's big but mostly because of converting the test file to use opaque pointers and then adding a few additional test cases. So most of the diff should just be NFC test improvements. 

3e6cff23e9bccc3b4a2f726eff8f4debe612a1b2 is the real functional change, the code change is very small and purely restricts existing behavior.

comments.author: tru
comments.body: Ok! I'll queue it for a merge then!

issue.title: [LoopVectorize] Diff check is not emitted correctly in nested loop

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define void @nested_loop_outer_iv_addrec_invariant_in_inner2(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %outer.header

outer.header:                                     ; preds = %outer.latch, %entry
  %outer.iv = phi i64 [ %outer.iv.next, %outer.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.a = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %a, i64 %outer.iv
  br label %inner.body

inner.body:                                       ; preds = %inner.body, %outer.header
  %inner.iv = phi i64 [ 0, %outer.header ], [ %inner.iv.next, %inner.body ]
  %l = load i32, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  %sub = sub i32 %l, 10
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %inner.iv
  store i32 %sub, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %inner.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %inner.iv, 1
  %inner.cond = icmp eq i64 %inner.iv.next, %n
  br i1 %inner.cond, label %outer.latch, label %inner.body

outer.latch:                                      ; preds = %inner.body
  %outer.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %outer.iv, 1
  %outer.cond = icmp eq i64 %outer.iv.next, %n
  br i1 %outer.cond, label %exit, label %outer.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %outer.latch
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define void @nested_loop_outer_iv_addrec_invariant_in_inner1(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %outer.header

outer.header:                                     ; preds = %outer.latch, %entry
  %outer.iv = phi i64 [ %outer.iv.next, %outer.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.a = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %a, i64 %outer.iv
  br label %inner.body

inner.body:                                       ; preds = %inner.body, %outer.header
  %inner.iv = phi i64 [ 0, %outer.header ], [ %inner.iv.next, %inner.body ]
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %inner.iv
  %l = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %sub = sub i32 %l, 10
  store i32 %sub, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  %inner.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %inner.iv, 1
  %inner.cond = icmp eq i64 %inner.iv.next, %n
  br i1 %inner.cond, label %outer.latch, label %inner.body

outer.latch:                                      ; preds = %inner.body
  %outer.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %outer.iv, 1
  %outer.cond = icmp eq i64 %outer.iv.next, %n
  br i1 %outer.cond, label %exit, label %outer.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %outer.latch
  ret void
}
